title: OKX Exchange Migration — replace Bitget with OKX candle data provider
summary: Migrated from Bitget to OKX exchange due to Bitget IP-blocked endpoints. Implemented full OKX support including REST client, WebSocket client, backfill service, live collector, CLI, and validation tool.

deliverables.description: OKX REST API client — fetches candles with pagination using OKX's after-based cursor. Converts OKX candle format [ts,o,h,l,c,vol,...] to normalized Candle model. Supports all granularities (1m, 5m, 15m, 30m, 1H, 4H, 1D). Rate limiting with tenacity retry.

OKX WebSocket client — connects to wss://ws.okx.com:8443/ws/v5/public, subscribes to candlestick channel, dispatches parsed Candle objects to handlers. Includes auto-reconnect with exponential backoff and ping keepalive.
OKX backfill service — paginates through OKX history using after-cursor backward pagination. Gap detection, idempotent upsert via CandleRepository, structured OKXBackfillResult with stats.
OKXLiveCollector — persistent WebSocket session that subscribes to XAU-USDT-SWAP 15m candles, dispatches to handler, and persists each candle via CandleRepository.upsert_many. Includes retry logic.
Added --exchange bitget/okx flag to CLI backfill. Supports both exchanges from same command.
Validation tool — fetches candles from OKX API, compares against database records (open_price, high, low, close with 0.01% tolerance), reports match/mismatch counts.

bitget_reason: All Bitget public endpoints (history-candles, kline, etc.) return 400172 Parameter verification failed. Only /tickers without query params works. Root cause: IP blocked by Bitget (likely cloud/datacenter IP block or geo-restriction). All 10 residential proxies from datacenter IPs also blocked.
